common.title: Common Settings
common.privacyModeDescription: Stop automatic outbound requests owned by Biochat:
common.privacyModeAcpRegistry: ACP Registry refresh and icon sync
common.privacyModeManualActions: Manual checks and manual refresh actions stay available.

autoCompaction.title: Auto Compaction
autoCompaction.description: When a conversation approaches the model context window limit, Biochat can summarize older history automatically so the session keeps going. If disabled, overflowing history is dropped directly instead of summarized.
autoCompaction.thresholdDescription: Start compaction when prompt usage reaches this share of the effective history budget. Lower values create more headroom; higher values preserve more raw context.
autoCompaction.retainPairsDescription: Keep the most recent completed user and assistant pairs outside compaction. The current query is not included.

common.invalidProxyUrl: Invalid proxy URL, please enter valid http/https URL
common.addCustomSearchEngineDesc: Add a new search engine by providing a name and search URL. The URL must include {query} as the query placeholder.
common.searchEngineUrlError: URL must include {'{'}query{'}'} as the query placeholder
common.deleteCustomSearchEngineDesc: Are you sure you want to delete the custom search engine "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
common.testSearchEngineDesc: A test search for "weather" will be performed using the {engine} search engine.
common.testSearchEngineNote: If the search page requires login or other actions, you can perform them in the test window. Please close the test window when you're done.
common.contentProtectionEnableDesc: Prevent screen sharing apps from capturing the Biochat window to help protect your privacy. Not all apps honor this setting; in some environments a black window may remain.
common.contentProtectionDisableDesc: Allow screen sharing apps to capture the Biochat window.
common.contentProtectionRestartNotice: Changing this setting will restart the application. Do you want to continue?
common.loggingEnableDesc: Enabling logging will help us diagnose issues and improve the application. Log files may contain sensitive information.
common.loggingDisableDesc: Disabling logging will stop collecting application logs.
common.loggingRestartNotice: Changing this setting will restart the application. Do you want to continue?
common.notificationsDesc: When Biochat is not in the foreground, if a session is generated, a system notification will be sent
common.fileMaxSizeHint: Limits the maximum size of a single uploaded file

deepchatAgents.title: Biochat Agents
deepchatAgents.description: Manage Biochat agents and the defaults they seed into new sessions.
deepchatAgents.name: Name
deepchatAgents.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional note about what this agent is for
deepchatAgents.avatarDefaultDesc: Biochat keeps the built-in icon. Other agents use a text avatar.
deepchatAgents.avatarLucideDesc: Pick a Lucide icon and set light/dark colors.
deepchatAgents.avatarMonogramDesc: Use two Latin letters or the first CJK character.
deepchatAgents.systemPromptPlaceholder: Leave blank for an empty system prompt
deepchatAgents.defaultProjectPathPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the global default directory
deepchatAgents.subagentsDescription: Configure the reusable child-session slots exposed to subagent_orchestrator.
deepchatAgents.compactionDescription: These defaults only affect future Biochat sessions.
deepchatAgents.deleteConfirm: Delete agent "{name}"? Its history will fall back to the built-in Biochat agent.

commands.title: Hooks Commands
commands.description: Run custom commands for the selected lifecycle events.
commands.hint: The full payload is sent to stdin as JSON.
commands.name: Name
commands.empty: No hooks yet. Add one to get started.
commands.guideDescription: When a checked event fires, Biochat runs the command. The current event is available from stdin JSON, environment variables, and command placeholders.
commands.deliveryStdin: The full payload is written to stdin as JSON.
commands.deliveryPlaceholder: You can place command placeholders directly in the command line.
commands.deliveryEnv: The same core metadata is also exposed as environment variables.
commands.metadataOnly: Command placeholders are best for small metadata. Read prompt/tool details, permission, usage, stop, and error fields from stdin JSON.
commands.placeholdersDescription: Placeholders are expanded before execution, so they can be passed as command arguments.

fields.isTest: Whether this run comes from the Test button
fields.workdir: Resolved working directory for the session
fields.messageId: Current user message ID when available
fields.toolCallId: Current tool call ID when available

data.title: Data Settings
data.backupSelectDescription: Choose the backup snapshot to import.
data.noBackupsAvailable: No backups available. Run a backup first.
data.importConfirmDescription: Importing will overwrite all current data, including chat history and settings. Make sure you have backed up important data. Data will refresh automatically after import.
data.resetConfirmDescription: Please select the type of data to reset. This operation cannot be undone and the application will restart automatically after reset.
data.resetChatDataDesc: Delete all chat history and conversation records
data.resetKnowledgeDataDesc: Delete all knowledge base files and vector data
data.resetConfigDesc: Delete all app settings, model configurations and custom prompts
data.resetAllDesc: Delete all data including chat history, configurations and cache files
data.resetCompleteDevMessage: Please manually restart the application in development mode. Stop the current process and run pnpm run dev again
data.resetDataDescription: Reset chat, knowledge, config, or all data by type.

databaseRepair.title: Database Repair
databaseRepair.description: Detect and automatically repair the database
databaseRepair.toastSuggestedDescription: Biochat detected a schema problem ({reason}). You can run database repair now.
databaseRepair.toastManualDescription: Repaired {repaired} issue(s). {manual} issue(s) still need manual handling.
databaseRepair.toastFailedDescription: Unable to complete database repair right now. Please check the logs and try again.

modelConfigUpdate.title: Update Model Config
modelConfigUpdate.updatedDescription: The latest provider metadata has been refreshed.
modelConfigUpdate.upToDateDescription: Your local model config is already using the latest provider metadata.
modelConfigUpdate.failedDescription: Unable to refresh model config right now. Please try again later.

yoBrowser.clearFailedDescription: Please try again or check the logs for more information.
yoBrowser.clearedDescription: The sandbox session has been reset and the associated tab will be reloaded.
yoBrowser.confirmDescription: The YoBrowser session's cookies, cache, LocalStorage, IndexedDB and other data will be deleted, and you may need to log in to the website again.
yoBrowser.description:
An independent browser sandbox that does not share cookies and local storage with the main application. 
YoBrowser data can be cleared with one click here.
yoBrowser.title: YoBrowser Sandbox

dashboard.title: Usage Dashboard
dashboard.description: Track token usage, cache hits, and estimated cost. Token and cost figures are estimated from public information, and some providers or earlier messages may be incomplete, so treat these figures as reference only.

backfill.runningDescription: Usage data is still being updated in the background. Current results are already available.
backfill.failedDescription: Refresh this page or try again later.

empty.title: No usage stats yet
empty.description: Token usage, cache hits, and estimated cost will appear here once usage data is available.
empty.historyNote: Token and cost figures are estimated from public information. Some providers or earlier messages may be incomplete, so treat these figures as reference only.

summary.totalTokensDescription: Shows how input and output tokens contribute to the total.
summary.cachedTokensDescription: Prompt tokens served from provider cache.
summary.cacheHitRateDescription: Cached prompt tokens divided by total input tokens.
summary.estimatedCostDescription: Estimated in USD using provider pricing or AIHubMix fallback pricing.
summary.estimatedCostTrendEmpty: No cost recorded in the last 30 days.
summary.recordingStartedAtDescription: The earliest usage record currently stored in this dashboard.
summary.withBiochatDaysSentence: You are on day {days} with Biochat.
summary.withBiochatDaysDescription: Based on your earliest usage record from {date}.
summary.nostalgiaDaysDetail: You and Biochat have spent {days} days together.
summary.nostalgiaMostActiveDayDetail: {date} was your most active day, with {count} messages.

calendar.title: Contribution Calendar
calendar.description: Daily token usage over the last 365 days.

breakdown.providerDescription: Ranked by estimated cost, then by total tokens.
breakdown.modelDescription: Top 10 models by estimated cost, then by total tokens.

rtk.title: RTK Savings
rtk.description: Estimated tokens prevented from reaching the model context by RTK during Biochat native command execution.
rtk.descriptionDisabled: RTK is turned off for this app session, so commands run through the normal execution path.
rtk.descriptionChecking: Biochat is verifying whether RTK can run correctly in the current environment.
rtk.descriptionHealthy: RTK is active for Biochat native command execution.
rtk.descriptionUnhealthy: RTK failed its startup health check and has been disabled for this app session.

systemPrompt.label: System Prompt
systemPrompt.description: Set the system prompt for the AI assistant to define its behavior and role

temperature.label: Model Temperature
temperature.description: Controls the randomness of the output; higher values produce more creative responses

contextLength.label: Context Length
contextLength.description: Set the maximum length of the conversation context

responseLength.label: Response Length
responseLength.description: Set the maximum length of the AI response

artifacts.description: Enabling the Artifacts feature allows the AI to generate richer content
artifacts.title: Artifacts

contextLength.description: Set the context length that the model can handle
contextLength.label: Context Length

modelConfig.description: Please note that this configuration is only valid for the current model and will not affect other models. Please modify it with caution. Incorrect parameters may cause the model to not work properly.

name.label: Model Name
name.description: The display name of the model

id.label: Model ID
id.description: The unique identifier of the model
id.duplicate: A model with this ID already exists

functionCall.description: Whether the model supports function calls natively (Biochat will automatically simulate function calls after turning off this option)
functionCall.label: Function Calls

maxTokens.description: Set the maximum number of tokens for a single output of the model
maxTokens.label: Maximum output length

reasoningToggle.description: Controls whether reasoning is enabled by default for this model
reasoningToggle.label: Enable reasoning

interleavedThinking.label: Interleaved Thinking
interleavedThinking.description: Controls whether this model handles reasoning and tool-call messages with Interleaved Thinking. Provider defaults are applied automatically.

thinkingBudget.label: Thinking Budget
thinkingBudget.description: Set the maximum token length of the thinking process to control reasoning depth

validation.minValue: Thinking budget value is below the minimum

resetConfirm.message:
Are you sure you want to reset the configuration of this model to the default? 
This operation is irrevocable.
resetConfirm.title: Confirm reset

reasoningEffort.label: Reasoning Effort
reasoningEffort.description: Controls the depth of model reasoning; higher effort produces better results but slower responses

verbosity.label: Verbosity
verbosity.description: Controls the detail level and length of model responses

temperature.description: Control the randomness of the output. Most models are 0-1, and some support between 0-2. Higher values increase randomness.
temperature.fixedMoonshotKimi: Moonshot Kimi K2.5/K2.6 fixes temperature to {enabled} when thinking is enabled and {disabled} when it is disabled.
temperature.label: Temperature

modelConfig.title: Custom model parameters

validation.contextLengthMin: The context length must be greater than 0
validation.contextLengthRequired: The context length cannot be empty
validation.maxTokensMax: The maximum output length cannot exceed 1000000
validation.maxTokensMin: The maximum output length must be greater than 0
validation.maxTokensRequired: The maximum output length cannot be empty
validation.temperatureMax: The temperature must be less than or equal to 2
validation.temperatureMin: The temperature must be greater than or equal to 0

vision.description: Does the model support visual ability?

reasoningVisibility.label: Reasoning Visibility
reasoningVisibility.description: Controls whether reasoning content is omitted or shown as summarized notes when reasoning is enabled

timeout.description: Set the timeout for a single model request. If exceeded, the request will be aborted.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Review project directories used by your sessions, open them quickly, or set one as the default for new chats.

default.description: New chats preselect this directory unless you manually choose a different one.

history.title: History
history.description: Only directories that were actually used by sessions are listed here.

temp.title: Temp Directories
temp.description: Directories under temp roots or app-managed workspaces are grouped here and hidden by default.

remote.title: Remote
remote.description: Configure Telegram, Feishu / Lark, QQBot, Discord, and WeChat iLink remote control in one place.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.description: Connect a Telegram bot for direct messages, groups, and topic-thread remote control.
telegram.botTokenDescription: Enter the Telegram bot token used for Telegram remote control.

feishu.title: Feishu / Lark
feishu.description: Connect a Feishu bot for direct messages, group chats, and topic threads.
feishu.encryptKeyPlaceholder: Leave empty unless event encryption is enabled
feishu.accessRulesDescription: Pair in a direct message first. In groups, only paired users who {'@'} mention the bot can drive a session.
feishu.accessRule1: Pair in a direct message with /pair [code].
feishu.accessRule2: In groups and topics, the bot only reacts when a paired user {'@'} mentions it.

discord.title: Discord
discord.description: Connect a Discord bot for DMs, channel mentions, and slash-command remote control.
discord.remoteControlDescription: Discord remote control uses its own bot token, gateway, and REST flow.
discord.accessRule1: Send /pair [code] in a DM or target channel to authorize the current Discord endpoint.
discord.accessRule2: Channel messages only enter the conversation when the bot is mentioned, which avoids absorbing regular chat.

remoteControl.description: Bind each remote endpoint to one session and keep the session available across commands.
remoteControl.defaultWorkdirHelper: Workspace directory for remote sessions. Required when the default agent is ACP.
remoteControl.pairDialogDescription: Generate a pairing code and finish pairing from your {channel} bot chat.
remoteControl.pairDialogInstructionTelegram: Send this command to the Telegram bot:
remoteControl.pairDialogInstructionFeishu: Send this command to the Feishu bot in a direct message:
remoteControl.pairDialogInstructionDiscord: Send this command to the Discord bot in a DM or target channel:
remoteControl.bindingsDialogDescription: Remove remote endpoint bindings one by one.
remoteControl.pairingSuccessDescription: The paired principal list has been refreshed.
remoteControl.pairDialogInstructionQQBot: Send this command to the QQ bot chat or authorized QQ group:
remoteControl.authorizedPrincipalsDescription: These {channel} accounts are authorized to pair and control sessions.
remoteControl.sessionBindingsDescription: Each remote endpoint below is currently bound to one Biochat session.

hooks.title: Telegram Hooks
hooks.description: Reuse the same Telegram bot for hook notifications.

qqbot.title: QQBot
qqbot.description: Connect an official QQ Bot for C2C chats and authorized group mention-based bot control.
qqbot.remoteControlDescription: QQ official openid identifiers differ between C2C and groups, so direct messages pair users while groups are authorized separately with /pair.

weixinIlink.title: WeChat iLink
weixinIlink.description: Connect an official WeChat iLink Bot through QR login, then route remote control sessions through the built-in adapter.
weixinIlink.loginDescription: Click the button below to open the official WeChat login window. Complete the iLink Bot authorization there, and the connected account will be added here automatically.
weixinIlink.ownerOnlyNotice: This first version only allows the QR-login owner account to control Biochat.
weixinIlink.loginDialogDescription: The official WeChat login page opens in a separate window. Complete the QR authorization there and keep this dialog open until the connection finishes.
weixinIlink.accountsDescription: Each connected iLink Bot account runs independently and keeps its own bindings and runtime state.
weixinIlink.noAccounts: No WeChat iLink accounts connected yet.
weixinIlink.remoteControlDescription: Set the default agent shared by WeChat iLink accounts, then manage the bindings created by incoming sessions.
weixinIlink.loginWindowOpened: The official WeChat login window has been opened. Complete the QR authorization there.
weixinIlink.loginSessionExpired: This login session has expired. Reopen the login window and try again.
weixinIlink.loginResponseIncomplete: The WeChat iLink login response was incomplete. Please try again.
weixinIlink.loginTimedOut: Timed out waiting for WeChat iLink authorization. Reopen the login window and try again.
weixinIlink.loginConnected: The WeChat iLink account has been connected.

ima.title: IMA Knowledge Base
ima.description: Connect to Tencent IMA knowledge base and retrieve content directly in conversation.
ima.connectSuccessDetail: IMA knowledge base connected. You can now use knowledge base tools in conversations.
ima.serverNotStarted: IMA server failed to start. Please check your credentials.
ima.step1: Open ima.qq.com in browser and login
ima.step2: Click avatar → "Claw Config" to enter configuration
ima.step3: Copy the Client ID and API Key from the page
ima.step4: Paste credentials above and click "Test Connection"
ima.toolQuery: Search content in a specific knowledge base (semantic search)
ima.toolInfo: Get detailed information about a knowledge base
ima.toolBrowse: Browse files and folders in a knowledge base
